name: Agent S
tagline: Open framework for AI agents that use your computer like a human
seoTitle: Agent S: Open-Source Computer-Use AI Agent Framework
metaDescription: Agent S is an open-source framework for building GUI agents that control your computer like a human. Runs on Linux, macOS, and Windows via gui-agents.
overview: Agent S is an open-source framework from Simular for building autonomous agents that interact with a computer through its graphical interface, the same way a person does. It looks at a screenshot of the screen, decides what to do, and produces actions such as clicks and keystrokes to carry out a task.

The project ships as the `gui-agents` Python library and runs on Linux, macOS, and Windows. Its latest version, Agent S3, reported a 72.60% score on the OSWorld benchmark, which the authors describe as the first result to pass human-level performance on that test.

A core idea in Agent S is splitting the work between a main reasoning model that plans the task and a separate grounding model that turns those plans into precise on-screen coordinates and executable actions. This lets you mix and match different model providers for each role.
features: Controls real desktop applications by reading screenshots and issuing mouse and keyboard actions through an Agent-Computer Interface
Splits work between a main planning model and a dedicated grounding model (such as UI-TARS-1.5-7B) for accurate clicking
Cross-platform support for Linux, macOS, and Windows from a single `gui-agents` install
Works with many model providers, including OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Azure OpenAI, Open Router, and self-hosted vLLM endpoints
Optional local coding environment that lets the agent run Python and Bash for tasks like file manipulation and data processing
Usable from a command-line tool (`agent_s`) or directly through the Python SDK for custom inference loops

gettingStarted.intro: Agent S installs as the gui-agents Python package. You need a main model API key, a grounding model endpoint, and Tesseract installed. The agent runs Python code to control your machine, so use it carefully on a single-monitor setup.

steps.heading: Install the library
steps.body: Install the published package, then install the Tesseract OCR engine that the grounding pipeline depends on.
steps.code:
pip install gui-agents
brew install tesseract

steps.heading: Set your API keys
steps.body: Export the keys for the providers you plan to use. You can put these in your shell profile or set them in a script.

steps.heading: Run the CLI agent
steps.body: Start Agent S3 from the command line, giving it a main model plus a grounding model endpoint. The recommended pairing is a strong reasoning model with UI-TARS-1.5-7B for grounding.
steps.code:
agent_s \
    --provider openai \
    --model gpt-5-2025-08-07 \
    --ground_provider huggingface \
    --ground_url http://localhost:8080 \
    --ground_model ui-tars-1.5-7b \
    --grounding_width 1920 \
    --grounding_height 1080

steps.heading: Or drive it from Python
steps.body: Import the agent and grounding classes, capture a screenshot, and call predict to get the next action to run.
steps.code:
from gui_agents.s3.agents.agent_s import AgentS3
from gui_agents.s3.agents.grounding import OSWorldACI

instruction = "Close VS Code"
info, action = agent.predict(instruction=instruction, observation=obs)
exec(action[0])

useCases: Automating multi-step desktop workflows across real applications without writing app-specific scripts
Building and researching computer-use agents that are benchmarked on OSWorld, WindowsAgentArena, and AndroidWorld
Letting an agent operate GUI software that has no API, by clicking and typing through the interface
Adding an optional local coding environment so the agent can run Python or Bash for file and data tasks
